programmes.show_description: Martin Geissler hears from the Scottish Information Commissioner about his belief there is no good reason the 'Salmond files' should not be released.
Joining Radio 5 Live.
News from across Scotland and the world, plus sport, weather, entertainment and big name interviews.
Stephen Jardine gets to the heart of the stories making the news.
News updates from Scotland and beyond, plus all your lunchtime sport, travel and weather.
Des Clarke hosts Scotland's award-winning satirical news quiz.
Nicola Meighan brings you feelgood music and chat with celebrity guests.
Breaking down the day's talking points with comment and opinion, plus sport, travel and weather.
Chris Difford shares a favourite vinyl album, Grant counts down the top 10 albums this week in 1983 and there's a tribute to the great songwriter Neil Sedaka from Tam Cowan.
Phoebe and Steph's session-selection; some of the best from the last few months including Fourth Daughter, DOSS, Indoor Foxes, Tina Sandwich, Pedalo, Lil Bon and Katherine Aly.
The best company to end the day, with lively chat and some of your favourite tunes.
Joining Radio 5 Live.
Mark Stephen and Rachel Stewart with stories from the great outdoors.
The stories that matter across Scotland and the world.
Amy Irons and Steven Mill kick-start your Saturday with lots of laughs, great guests and timeless tunes as we look back on the week's news and preview the day's football fixtures.
The most petty and ill-informed football show on radio.
We're live at Murrayfield, where Scotland host France in their fourth Six Nations game of the tournament.
Sportsound brings you all the latest football news and commentary from Dens Park, where Dundee host Motherwell in the Scottish Premiership at 3pm.
We're live at Hampden Park where Scotland play Luxembourg in the Women's World Cup qualifiers.
Scottish Cup holders Aberdeen travel to Dunfermline to fight for a place in the semi-finals, with all the action live on Sportsound.
Roddy Hart with a mix of classic tracks and new discoveries.
